titleStmt.title: SII 28.210: original edition by J. Sundaram
No. 210. (A.R. No. 365 of 1913.)

summary.p: CHIDAMBARAM, CHIDAMBARAM TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
Naṭarāja temple, west gōpuram. left of entrance.

Contains three Tamil verses. The first verse praises the victory of Vikkiramapāṇḍya over Vēṇāḍan. The second verse addresses the same the king who is refered to the as Bhuvanēka-vīra and Koṟkai-kāvalaṉ and refers to (Kākatīya) Gaṇapati and his daughter. The third suggests that Vikkirama-pāṇḍiya did not go (further) north (during his compaign of conquest) because there was a woman (Rudrāmbā) (ruling in that direction).

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī § Entumaruviyiravi puraviyiṉmuṉ
pūntu valaivīcum potiyile karantu ciṉave
ṇāṭaṉai veṉṟa vikkiramapāṇṭiyaṉ meyppū
ṇāram pūṇṭāṉ poruppu § puyalun taruvum
porukaip puvanekavīra puṉal vayalun taraḷanta
ru koṟkai kāvala vāraṇappor muyaluṅ ka
ṇapati moytta ceñcoti mukattiraṇṭu kaya
luṇṭeṉu matuvo muṉivāṟiya kāraṇame §
veṅkaṇ matayāṉai vikkirama pāṇṭiyaṉe
poṅki vaṭaticaiyiṟ pokāteyaṅ kiruppāḷ
peṇṇeṉṟu mīṇṭa perumāḷe pericai yā
ḻp paṇṇoṉṟum veyvāy pakai §
Digital edition of SII 28.210 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
